q: A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two parallel metal plates separated by air or other insulating material.
Charging a capacitor means connecting the capacitor to a ___.
What is the formula for surface charge density $\sigma$ on each plate?
If the plates are very close together, the electric field between the plates is uniform.
The value of the electric field between the plates is expressed as $E = \frac{\sigma}{\epsilon}$, where $\epsilon$ is the ___ of the insulating material.
The magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals $E d$.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to what?
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is inversely proportional to the separation of the plates.
The permittivity of vacuum $\epsilon_0$ is equal to ___ $C^2\ N^{-1}m^{-2}$.
What is the dielectric constant $\kappa$ for vacuum?
For all insulating materials other than vacuum, the dielectric constant $\kappa > 1$.
The dielectric constant $\kappa$ is the ratio of its permittivity to that of ___.
The dielectric constant can be defined as the ratio of two capacitances: $\kappa = \frac{C}{C_0}$.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected from a battery and a dielectric is inserted, the potential difference:
Since the charge $Q$ of the capacitor does not change when disconnected, a decrease in $V$ indicates an increase in capacitance.
Dielectric constant can be expressed as the ratio of capacitances as well as the ratio of ___.
Which of the following is an advantage provided by a dielectric?
a: Possible mechanical support between the plates

q: Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ greater than unity.
A dielectric increases the maximum operating voltage of a capacitor even though the physical ___ of the capacitor doesn't change.
Which material has a dielectric constant of 81 at $18\ ^{\circ}C$?
The permittivity $\epsilon$ of an insulating material is related to that of a vacuum $\epsilon_0$ as $\epsilon = \kappa \epsilon_0$.
The dielectric constant of air at 1 atm is ___.
Mica has a dielectric constant range of 3-6.
The dielectric strength of a dielectric is generally ___ than that of air.
Inserting a dielectric allows plates to be close together without electrical contact.
